PREGUNTAS:
1. How does the Insurance Contract Act, specifically Law 50/80 of October 8, impact the exclusions and clauses limiting the rights of the insured in the healthcare policy's General Conditions?
2. What significance does the provision to bold the exclusions and clauses limiting rights have under the Insurance Contract Act (Law 50/80 of 8 October) within the healthcare policy framework?
3. Can you explain the legal rationale behind the requirement to highlight exclusions and clauses limiting insured rights in bold, as stipulated by the Insurance Contract Act, Law 50/80?
---
Healthcare
In accordance with the provisions of the Insurance Contract Act 
(Law 50/80 of 8 October), the exclusions and clauses limiting the 
rights of the insured contained in the General Conditions of this 
policy are worded in bold.

PREGUNTAS:
1. In case the content of the policy differs from the proposition of insurance or agreed clauses, what is the timeframe within which the Policyholder or Insured can claim for corrections to be made by the Insurance Company?

2. How does the Insurance Contract Act (Law 50/80 of October 8) and the Insurance and Reinsurance Entity Organization, Supervision and Solvency Act (Law 20/2015 of July 14) relate to the governing framework of the insurance policy offered by the Insurer?

3. Under what conditions does the insurance policy automatically terminate the benefits of healthcare services for the Insured?
---
General Conditions
3
General Conditions
INTRODUCTORY ARTICLE
The products called “HEALTHCARE ASSISTANCE” include different benefits that can be 
taken out upon request in two health insurance policies. The subscribed benefits of the Pol­
icyholder regarding each Insured party are specified in the Specific Conditions of the policy 
and/or on the Individual Insurance Certificate.
ARTICLE 1. LEGAL FRAMEWORK GOVERNING THE POLICY
This insurance contract is governed by the provisions set forth in the General, Specific and 
Special Terms and Conditions, and its Appendices and Supplements, the Individual Insur­
ance Certificate, if applicable, and, unless agreed upon otherwise, in the manner most ben­
eficial for the Insured, by the Insurance Contract Act (Law 50/80 of October 8) and the Insur­
ance and Reinsurance Entity Organization, Supervision and Solvency Act (Law 20/2015 of 
July 14) and the implementing regulations thereof.
MAPFRE ESPAÑA, COMPAÑÍA DE SEGUROS Y REASEGUROS, S.A., hereinafter the 
Insurer, has a registered address in Spain and its activity is supervised and monitored by 
the Ministry of Economy and Competitiveness of Spain through the General Directorate for 
Insurance and Pension Funds.
If the content of the policy differs from the proposition of insurance or the agreed clauses, 
the Policyholder, or the Insured party as applicable, may claim to the Insurance Company, 
within a month of the delivery of the policy, for it to remedy the existing difference. If no claim 
is made within this period, the provisions of the policy shall apply.
ARTICLE 2. PURPOSE AND EXTENSION OF THE INSURANCE
Within the limits and conditions set forth in the policy, in accordance with the benefits taken 
out by the Policyholder and regarding the risks described in these General Conditions, and 
by means of payment of the premium and the Insured’s co-pay, applicable to each case, the 
Insurer undertakes as follows:
To directly assume the cost of medical, surgical and hospital care required by the Insured 
party due to illness or injury, including care derived from traffic accidents and/or occupational 
accidents and/or illnesses. This care shall be provided through the services approved by the 
Insurance Company for that purpose.
Optional monetary compensations may never be granted in lieu of providing health­
care assistance services.
The benefits of the policy shall be valid provided that the usual address of residence 
of the Insured is in Spain. If the Insured moves outside Spain, the benefits of the pol­
icy shall be automatically terminated. 
Healthcare

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cuáles son los beneficios médicos que se incluyen específicamente para el cuidado infantil según el Programa del Niño Sano y hasta qué edad se proporciona este servicio?

2. ¿Qué procedimientos son necesarios para que un asegurado reciba atención de enfermería o medios diagnósticos bajo las condiciones generales de este seguro, y en qué situaciones se permite que estos servicios se presten en el domicilio del asegurado?

3. ¿Cómo puede un asegurado seleccionar y acceder a los servicios médicos aprobados por la compañía de seguros para recibir cuidados primarios como medicina general o pediatría?
---
General Conditions
4
ARTICLE 3. DESCRIPTION OF HEALTHCARE ASSISTANCE 
BENEFITS
The benefits shall be stated in the Specific Conditions and/or the Individual Insurance Certif­
icate, and shall be provided solely through the Services Approved by the Insurance Compa­
ny, pursuant to the policy’s Specific Conditions, these General Conditions and its Appendix­
es and, where appropriate, the Special Conditions.
The following benefits can be taken out:
3.1. NON-HOSPITAL BENEFITS
These benefits include doctor’s consultations, minor outpatient surgery, and the diagnostic 
and/or therapeutic procedures that are specifically detailed below, as agreed in these Gen­
eral Conditions and in the Specific Conditions and/or on the Individual Insurance Certificate.
Non-hospital benefits include home and outpatient emergency care. 
3.1.1. Primary Care
What is covered
Healthcare assistance at the doctor’s office and/or the home of the Insured indicated in the 
policy, covering the following services:
•	 General Medicine.
•	 Pediatrics and Infant Care. This includes the care of children up to 14 years old and 
the preventive medicine program called Healthy Child Program (Programa del Niño 
Sano). This primary care program is mainly intended for all children from the time they 
are born up to 11 years old, and includes:
–
– Neonatal examination and early detection of anomalies.
–
– Regular health check-ups to follow up on nutrition and psychophysical development.
–
– Vaccinations according to the official schedule.
–
– Record of detected incidents and illnesses.
–
– Promotion and education for child health.
•	 Diagnostic means: Basic clinical analysis and conventional radiology without contrast 
dye.
•	 Nursing.
•	 Home and outpatient emergency care through Approved Services. 
How the service is provided
The Insured party may choose and make an appointment directly with any Service Approved 
by the Insurance Company.
General medicine, pediatrics and infant care, as well as nursing services, include care at the 
office or home of the Insured. The latter case shall only occur when, at the discretion of the 
doctor, the patient may not move due to the illness or injury he/she has.
The use of diagnostic means and the nursing service will require the written prescription from 
a doctor included in the Approved Services agreed by the Insurer, indicating, for the nursing 
service, if the care is at the office or home.

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cuáles son las condiciones para recibir cuidado médico en el hogar cuando se selecciona un médico general, pediatra o enfermera registrada que no reside en la localidad del Asegurado?

2. ¿Qué tipo de procedimientos están incluidos en la atención especializada ambulatoria y qué estudios genéticos específicamente están cubiertos según la póliza?

3. ¿Qué requisitos debe cumplir el Asegurado en caso de necesitar atención médica de emergencia en un centro médico y cómo debe proceder si tiene dudas sobre qué hacer en una situación de emergencia?
---
General Conditions
5
For home care, the selection of a general doctor, pediatrician or RN outside the locality 
where the Insured lives will require the prior acceptance of the Approved Service.
Care for home emergencies will require prior authorization from the Insurer. In cases 
involving a temporary change of address, the authorization will be requested through 
the emergency telephone service of the Insurer. If emergency care is needed at a 
medical center, the Insured must directly go to an emergency center approved by the 
Insurer. In case of doubt, the Insured should contact the emergency telephone service 
of the Insurer.
3.1.2. Specialized Outpatient Care
What is covered
Care by doctors in the different medical specialties listed below, diagnostic and therapeutic 
procedures, and minor surgery that are exclusively provided on an outpatient basis. 
Specialized Outpatient Care also includes radiological contrasts used in diagnostic imaging 
examinations.
Covered Medical Specialties:
•	 Allergology.  Vaccines and autovaccines shall be paid for by the Insured party.
•	 Clinical analyses. 
•	 Pathological anatomy.
•	 Anesthesiology and resuscitation. The coverage shall only include anesthetics 
in outpatient surgical processes.
•	 Angiology and peripheral vascular surgery.  Varicose vein sclerotherapy is ex­
cluded.
•	 Digestive system. 
•	 Cardiology.
•	 Cardiovascular surgery.
•	 General surgery and digestive system surgery. 
•	 Maxillofacial surgery.
•	 Pediatric surgery.
•	 Plastic and reconstructive surgery. 
•	 Chest surgery.
•	 Dermatology.  
•	 Diagnostic imaging.
•	 Endocrinology and nutrition. 
•	 Genetics: This exclusively includes the acts that are part of the list in Appendix 
I and only when they are required within the framework of the clinical diagno­
sis, for the confirmation of a genetic diagnosis requiring the establishment of a 
specific treatment or its modification. The coverage expressly excludes genetic 
studies of relatives and those of a preventive nature.
•	 Geriatrics.
•	 Gynecology and Obstretics.  
•	 Hematology and hemotherapy. 
•	 Internal medicine.

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cuáles son las limitaciones especificadas en la cobertura de hospitalización psiquiátrica y tratamiento psico-terapéutico para condiciones adictivas según las políticas de la empresa?

2. ¿Qué restricciones se aplican para el procedimiento de cirugía refractiva en oftalmología bajo el plan médico proporcionado por la empresa, y existen excepciones a estas exclusiones?

3. ¿Cómo se determina la finalización del tratamiento de rehabilitación y de terapia del habla para empleados que han sufrido un daño cerebral adquirido, y cuáles son los límites en la duración de esta cobertura?
---
General Conditions
6
•	 Nuclear medicine. 
•	 Nephrology. 
•	 Pulmonology. 
•	 Neurosurgery.
•	 Clinical neurophysiology.
•	 Neurology.
•	 Dentistry/Stomatology. This only includes the treatment proposal, one annual 
mouth cleaning, plain X-rays and simple teeth extractions. Periodontics, en­
dodontics, fillings, orthodontics, dental prostheses, implants and other dental 
treatments are excluded, unless the Dental Benefit of this policy is taken out.
•	 Ophthalmology. Excludes photodynamic therapy, refractive surgery (myopia, 
astigmatism, hyperopia) and presbyopia surgery.
•	 Medical oncology. Only consultations are included.
•	 Radiation oncology. Only consultations are included. 
•	 Otolaryngology.
•	 Podiatry. This includes treatments for foot pathologies regarding benign surface tu­
mors and nail pathologies. This coverage includes four chiropody sessions per year, 
surgical treatment of ingrown nails and podiatric treatment of papilloma.
•	 Psychiatry. This only includes the consultation in acute or exacerbated psychia­
tric processes. Coverage does not include habit-breaking treatment for addic­
tive pathologies, as well as day hospitals for psychiatric hospitalization.
•	 Rehabilitation and speech therapy. This includes the necessary treatments until the 
Insured has attained total, or otherwise, maximum functional recovery, as a result of 
having entered the process in a state of unsurpassable stabilization or the treatment 
is constituted as maintenance or occupational therapy. A doctor designated by the 
Insurer will evaluate the functional recovery attained. As regards treatment for neu­
rological rehabilitation and other treatments specific to Acquired Brain Damage 
in outpatient circumstances, coverage is limited to one time per Insured party 
for the duration of the policy and up to a maximum of 90 days. 
•	 Rheumatology. 
•	 Pain management. 
•	 Traumatology and orthopedics.
•	 Urology. The coverage does not include prostate hyperthermia.
•	 Ventilation therapy, aerosol therapy, oxygen therapy. In consultation or at home 
according to the specifications of the doctor. CPAP, BIPAP and apnea monitors are 
excluded.
•	 Cryo-preservation of stem cells from the blood and tissue of the umbilical cord. 
Providing this service has been requested linked to at least one of the parents 
insured in the policy before the birth and the new born baby is included in the 
policy within 10 days of the birth, the Insurance Company will assume the payment 
on behalf of the Insured Parties of the annual maintenance quotas accrued during the 
time he/she remains in the parent's insurance policy up to a limit of 30 years.

PREGUNTAS:
1. In the context of specialized outpatient care, what specific conditions must be met for family planning services such as IUD implantation and vasectomies to be covered under the insurance policy?

2. If an insured individual resides in a province where there are no approved services provided by the insurer for preventive medicine programs, what steps must they follow to access these services in another province?

3. What specific criteria must a woman meet to be considered for the early diagnosis program of breast diseases, and what procedures are included in this program if a risk is identified?
---
General Conditions
7
How the service is provided
The Insured party may choose and make an appointment directly with the Services Ap­
proved by the Insurance Company throughout Spain, with the exception of specific ther­
apeutic methods or diagnostic tests that require obtaining express authorization from the 
Insurance Company for coverage and performance. The list of services subject to authoriza­
tion are included in the current Medical Care Guide.
Travel costs to see specialists or go to health centers are always borne by the Insured. 
3.1.2.1. Additional Coverages for Specialized Outpatient Care
This includes the preventive medicine programs designed by the Insurer which are indicated 
below. These are provided at centers specifically approved for that purpose and include 
medical actions, clinical examinations and the use of diagnostic means specifically aimed at 
preventing illnesses.
When there are no services approved by the Insurer in the province of residence of the In­
sured, these programs may be undertaken in the Approved Services of other provinces, after 
consultation with the Insurer.
•	 Family planning.
This program includes:
–
– IUD implantation techniques (including the cost of the conventional or standard IUD).
–
– Tubal ligation
–
– Vasectomy.
•	 Early diagnosis of breast diseases.
This program includes:
–
– Analysis of the risk of the Insured based on a questionnaire established for this purpose.
–
– Consultation and examination by a specialized doctor.
–
– Breast ultrasound.
–
– Clinical report with diagnosis, risk assessment and recommendations.
A mammogram will be performed if a situation of risk is detected after the initial tests.
The early diagnosis program of breast diseases is mainly intended for women over 40 years 
old who have not had children or who had their first child after 35, who have experienced 
menarche (appearance of the first period) before turning 12 years old, who experienced 
menopause after turning 50 years old, who are receiving hormone treatment, and who have 
a history of breast disease or a family history of breast cancer.
•	 Early diagnosis of gynecological diseases.
This program includes:
–
– Analysis of the risk of the Insured based on a questionnaire established for this purpose.
–
– Consultation and examination by a specialized doctor.
–
– Colposcopy and vaginal cytology.
–
– Gynecological ultrasound.

PREGUNTAS:
1. Considering the risk factors listed in the early diagnosis programs, how does the program prioritize eligibility for individuals who may have overlapping risk factors for multiple programs, such as a 50-year-old obese smoker with high blood pressure and a family history of both coronary heart disease and diabetes?

2. In the context of the early diagnosis program for gynecological diseases, are there any additional criteria or tests that might apply if a woman over 25 years old, who also meets the criteria for early menopause, wishes to be assessed for potential impacts on heart disease or diabetes, given her overall health profile?

3. For the early diagnosis and monitoring programs related to diabetes, are there any specific protocols in place that address the management of individuals who may simultaneously be enrolled in the coronary disease program due to overlapping risk factors like high blood pressure and cholesterol, to ensure comprehensive care without duplication of examinations?
---
General Conditions
8
–
– Specific analysis.
–
– Clinical report with diagnosis, risk assessment and recommendations.
The early diagnosis program of gynecological diseases is mainly intended for women over 
25 years old who were pregnant before turning 20 years old, who have genital infections, 
who experienced menarche (appearance of the first period) before turning 12 years old, who 
experienced menopause after turning 50 years old, who have general alterations of the peri­
od, and who have a family history of gynecological cancer or who are obese, have diabetes, 
hypertension or who smoke.
•	 Early diagnosis of coronary diseases.
This program includes:
–
– Analysis of the risk of the Insured based on a questionnaire established for this purpose.
–
– Consultation and examination by a specialized doctor.
–
– Electrocardiogram.
–
– Stress test.
–
– Specific analysis.
–
– Clinical report with diagnosis, risk assessment and recommendations.
An echocardiogram will be performed if a situation of risk is detected after the initial tests.
The program is mainly intended for people over 40 years old, men and women, with high 
cholesterol, hypertension, who smoke, have diabetes, are obese, have a sedentary lifestyle 
or have a family history of coronary heart disease.
•	 Early diagnosis of prostate diseases.
This program includes:
–
– Analysis of the risk of the Insured based on a questionnaire established for this purpose.
–
– Consultation and examination by a specialized doctor.
–
– Urological ultrasound.
–
– Specific analysis.
–
– Clinical report with diagnosis, risk assessment and recommendations.
The program is mainly intended for men who are over 50 years old and do or do not have 
symptoms when urinating, and who would like to know the condition of their prostate.
•	 Early diagnosis of diabetes and monitoring the diabetic patient.
This program includes:
–
– Early diagnosis of diabetes with consultation and examination by a specialist, 
electrocardiogram, ocular fundus, specific analysis, report and recommendations.
–
– Monitoring and treatment of diabetic patients with control and monitoring by a 
specialist, annual diabetes monitoring consultation with a personalized patient file, 
specific analysis and other diagnostic examinations. 
The early detection program of diabetes is intended for people with a family history of diabe­
tes; who are obese; who are over 40 years old; who have high blood pressure and cholester­
ol; who are excessively tired; and who experience an increase in appetite, thirst, the volume 
of urine expelled per day and weight loss.

PREGUNTAS:
1. How does the monitoring and treatment program for diabetic patients address the risk of glaucoma, particularly for those with a family history or those who have been on medications like antidepressants or corticosteroids?
  
2. For a child eligible for the early detection of deafness program, what are the requirements regarding the parents’ insurance policy, and what diagnostic tests are included in the program?

3. In terms of cardiac rehabilitation, what components and session limits are provided, and how does the program facilitate the reintegration of patients into their work and social environments?
---
General Conditions
9
The program for monitoring and treatment of diabetic patients is intended for all people di­
agnosed with diabetes.
•	 Early diagnosis of glaucoma.
This program includes:
–
– Risk assessment questionnaire.
–
– Consultation and examination by a doctor specialized in ophthalmology, examining 
visual acuity and the optic nerve and measuring intraocular pressure.
–
– Exam of the front part of the eye using a slit lamp.
–
– Clinical report with diagnosis, risk assessment and recommendations.
If an increase in intraocular pressure is detected and/or the optic nerve is affected, a gonios­
copy and campimetry will be performed to determine the state of the disease.
This program is mainly intended for people who have a family history of glaucoma; who are 
nearsighted; who have a cardiovascular disease, migraines or diabetes; who have been 
taking medicine that may cause intraocular pressure to increase in the last 2 years (mainly 
antidepressants or corticosteroids); or who are over 40 years old and have not had an oph­
thalmological exam in the last 3 years.
•	 Early detection of deafness in children.
This program includes:
–
– Consultation and examination.
–
– Otoacoustic emissions.
–
– Auditory evoked potentials of the brain stem.
This program is intended for Insured from birth up to 7 years of age. It is essential that the 
mother or father has had a policy for more than 8 months at the time of birth for this service 
to be provided.
This includes speech therapy sessions, up to a maximum limit of 20 sessions per in­
sured party per year.
•	 Cardiac rehabilitation.
This program includes:
–
– Consultation and evaluation by specialists.
–
– Cardiac rehabilitation sessions by means of physical exercises under cardiological 
control and monitoring, up to a maximum limit of 30 sessions per insured party 
per year.
–
– Follow-up cardiac stress test.
This includes rehabilitation programs intended for coronary patients and patients who have 
received cardiac surgical interventions for the purpose of improving their physical and psy­
chological condition, facilitating reincorporation of the patient in his/her work and social life 
as soon as possible.
Traveling Insured may receive part of the treatment at the rehabilitation center approved by 
the Insurer. The patient can undergo the rest of the treatment independently at his/her own

PREGUNTAS:
1. If an employee has an eating disorder, how many psychotherapy sessions are they entitled to through the insurance policy, and what steps must they take to access this coverage?

2. Can an insured employee access osteopathy services at any osteopath of their choice, and what financial responsibilities do they have in terms of deductibles?

3. How often can an adult employee undergo a biomechanical walking study covered by the insurance policy, and what is the primary purpose of this study?
---
General Conditions
10
home, following the protocols given by the center and under the supervision of the cardiolo­
gist responsible for the patient.
3.1.2.2. Special Services
●
●Brief psychotherapy or focal therapy treatments
What is covered
This services includes a consultation with the psychologist and treatment, up to a maximum 
limit of 20 sessions per Insured Party per year, with the exception of eating disorders, 
for which the maximum limit is 40 sessions per Insured Party per year.
How the service is provided
The Insured party must obtain prior authorization from the Insurance Company. Furthermore, 
the treatment must have been prescribed by a psychiatrist included in the Services Approved 
by the Insurance Company as additional treatment for psychiatric pathologies. The prescription 
shall always be accompanied by a report from the prescribing doctor, justifying that the 
requested treatment is within the coverages of the policy. There shall be a deductible to be 
paid by the Insured party. This can be found in Appendix I of these General Conditions. 
This deductible shall be reviewed annually and may vary depending on the variations 
in care costs after they have been reported by the Insured party.
●
●Osteopathy
What is covered
The treatment prescribed by the osteopath with a maximum of 8 sessions per insured 
party per year.
How the service is provided
The Insured party must go to an osteopath included in the Medical Care Guide after author­
ization by the Company. There shall be a deductible to be paid by the Insured party. 
This can be found in Appendix I of these General Conditions. This deductible shall be 
reviewed annually and may vary depending on the variations in care costs after they 
have been reported by the Insured party.
●
●Biomechanical walking study
What is covered
The study, using a specialized walking analysis system, observing possible alterations that 
could affect the skeletal structure, for the purpose of preventing injuries or correcting already 
detected problems.
In adults, this covers conducting one study every five years, and in children (up to 
14 years old) it covers conducting one study every two years.

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cuáles son los criterios específicos que deben cumplirse para que una pareja sea elegible para la cobertura de tratamientos de reproducción asistida según el fragmento?

2. ¿Qué tipos de procedimientos obstétricos especializados están cubiertos para el monitoreo del embarazo del asegurado y bajo qué condición se proporciona dicha asistencia?

3. ¿Cómo se determina y ajusta anualmente el deducible aplicable a las técnicas de ICSI o microinyección de esperma, y bajo qué sección se encuentra esta información en las Condiciones Generales?
---
General Conditions
11
How the service is provided
To conduct the study, the Insured must go to the centers recommended by the Insurer 
with a medical prescription.
Once the study is completed, if so desired, the Insured can request at his/her expense, dig­
italization of the study, which will provide a virtual mold of the foot in three dimensions, and 
the manufacture of customized insoles, and will have to pay for these at the center or 
service where they are made.
●
●Assisted reproduction
What is covered
The treatment and study of the sterility/infertility of the couple, artificial insemination tech­
niques and in vitro fertilization. Both members of the couple must be Insured Parties 
of the policy. They should not have undergone contraceptive surgery (vasectomy or 
tubal ligation) and one of the members must have a confirmed diagnosis of sterility. 
Neither one may have reached 40 years of age or have had children naturally or by 
means of assisted reproduction techniques. Coverage includes two attempts at arti­
ficial insemination and one attempt at "in-vitro" fertilization during the validity of the 
policy.
How the service is provided
In assisted reproduction, the specialist in assisted reproduction techniques designated by 
the Insurer will prescribe and select the treatment based on the corresponding sterility stud­
ies. The treatment will be provided at the medical centers and hospitals and by the doctors 
designated for this purpose by the Insurer. It is not necessary for these to be located in the 
province of residence of the Insured.
If ICSI or sperm microinjection techniques are applied, there will be a deductible to 
be paid by the Insured party. This can be found in Appendix I of these General Con­
ditions. This deductible shall be reviewed annually and may vary depending on the 
variations in care costs after they have been reported by the Insured party.
The application of assisted reproduction techniques will always be in compliance with cur­
rent legislation.
3.1.3. Specialized Obstetric Care
What is covered
Outpatient care by an obstetrician or gynecologist, the diagnostic and therapeutic proce­
dures for monitoring the pregnancy of the Insured, provided that hospitalization is not re­
quired. This assistance specifically covers the following in outpatient care and Services Ap­
proved for this purpose by the Insurer: cytologies, ultrasounds, laparoscopy, karyotypes, 
genotypes, amniocentesis, amnioscopy and maternal-fetal monitoring.
The following Preventive Medicine Programs are included within this category of assistance:

PREGUNTAS:
1. If a pregnant employee in their third trimester is seeking support for childbirth preparation, how many hours can they expect the complete treatment program to last according to the company policy?

2. Can a pregnant employee with diabetes or blood sugar alterations receive early diagnosis and educational support on managing diabetes through the company's insurance plan, and what does this entail?

3. If an employee requires hospitalization for a medical procedure in Spain, what specific hospital benefits and accommodations are provided according to the insurance policy, and which type of ambulance service will be covered?
---
General Conditions
12
•	 Preparation for giving birth.
This is mainly intended for pregnant women after the second trimester of pregnancy.  This in­
cludes the group of breathing and relaxation techniques, prenatal exercise and group psycho­
therapy, which are applied so the pregnant woman is physically and psychologically prepared 
for giving birth. The complete treatment shall have a maximum duration of 15 hours.
•	 Monitoring and treating diabetes during pregnancy
This is intended for pregnant diabetic women or women who, during their pregnancy, expe­
rience alterations in their blood sugar. This includes early diagnosis, interconsultation and 
education on diabetes, analysis and specific examinations.
How the service is provided
The Insured party may choose and make an appointment directly with the Services Approved 
by the Insurance Company throughout Spain, with the exception of specific therapeutic 
methods or diagnostic tests that require obtaining express authorization from the Insurance 
Company. The list of services subject to authorization can be found in the current 
Medical Care Guide.
3.2. HOSPITAL BENEFITS 
What is covered
This benefit includes the stay in a hospital of the Services Approved by the Insurance Com­
pany, with use of a single room, meals for the admitted Insured party, companion bed (if the 
hospital has them), use of the operating room, consumables, osteosynthesis materials, pros­
theses, implants (according to Appendix I of the General Conditions of the policy), medica­
tions, treatments and diagnostic tests, as well as the fees earned by the Services Approved 
by the Insurance Company that arise during the hospitalization.
Services provided by major outpatient surgery and in day hospitals, both for surgical and 
medical procedures (including medication, products and diagnostic tools used during the 
stay in the hospital), as agreed in these General and Specific Conditions, will be covered.
For the purpose of this contract, hospitalization is understood to mean admission of 
the patient and bed rest in the hospital for at least 24 hours, major outpatient surgery 
and day hospitalizations. 
The provision of hospital services shall always be adjusted to the characteristics and capa­
bilities of the hospital and its various special units.
Within this hospital benefit, the intensive care unit, coronary care unit, isolation units and 
home hospitalization are included, as well as conventional or emergency ambulance ser­
vice, in the corresponding hospital mode. This ambulance service shall be carried out by one 
of the Services Approved by the Insurance Company and shall consist of moving the Insured 
party to one of the hospital centers in the current Medical Care Guide.

Care Guide in force. In case of doubt, the Insured party should contact the emergency

telephone service of the Insurance Company, where he/she will be directed to the nearest

This service includes transfers by ordinary or emergency ambulances, in the cases specified 
below, as long as a qualified doctor orders it in writing and if physical circumstances 
prevent the patient from using ordinary transportation services (public services, taxi, 
own vehicle and/or similar). This service exclusively refers to land ambulances and

– Transfer of the Insured between hospitals.
–

How the service is provided
In general, for any type of hospitalization, prior authorization must be requested from the 
Insurer at least 48 hours in advance.
The prescription from the specialist for hospital care must be for an Approved Service agreed 
by the Insurer, stating the reason for admission.
In cases of emergency hospitalization, the written prescription or admission report issued 
by the Approved Service will suffice. In these cases, the Insured must identify him or herself 
to the administrative services of the clinic as a MAPFRE ESPAÑA insured party, and must 
inform the Insurer of the admission within a maximum period of 72 hours, for the purposes 
of obtaining the proper authorization.
If it is an extreme or life-threatening emergency, the Insured may be treated by the nearest 
doctor or hospital. In this case, the Insured or other person acting on his/her behalf must 
make contact in a reliable way (telegram, fax, or personal appearance) within 72 hours of 
care and/or admission, and obtain the authorization of the Insurer. They must also submit a 
medical report to justify the extreme or life-threatening emergency. Medical services espe­
cially designated by the Insurer may decide to transfer the Insured to an Approved Service, 
provided there is no medical contraindication and, in any case, to assess the severity of the 
emergency that prompted care by services not approved by the Insurer.
If the Insured or the person representing him/her does not accept the transfer mentioned 
in the preceding paragraph, the Insurer will not pay the expenses accrued from the time of 
notification. Likewise, the Insurer will not pay for expenses when the type of emergency does 
not justify the unapproved care services.
Home hospitalization service always requires obtaining prior authorization from the Insurer. 
The authorization shall be subject to the existence, in the locality of residence of the Insured, 
of an Approved Service that can provide the service. This exclusively includes medical care 
by a general doctor and the participation of nursing personnel for injections, fluid therapy, 
catheters and healing wounds.

An interconsultation with another specialist or hospital accredited worldwide is held in order

SUMMARY OF LEGAL REGULATIONS
1. EXTRAORDINARY EVENTS COVERED
a)	The following natural phenomena: earthquakes and tsunamis; extraordinary flooding 
(including that caused by breaking waves); volcanic eruptions; atypical cyclonic storms 
(including wind gusts over 120 km/h and tornadoes); and falling astral bodies and 
meteorites.
b)	Those occurring violently as a result of terrorism, rebellion, sedition, mutiny and pop­
ular tumult.
c)	Events or actions involving the Armed Forces or Security Forces in peacetime.
Atmospheric and seismic events, volcanic eruptions and falling sidereal bodies shall be cer­
tified, at the request of the Insurance Compensation Consortium, through reports issued 
by the State Meteorological Agency (AEMET), the National Geographic Institute and other 
competent public bodies in this area. In cases of events of a political or social nature, and in 
the event of damage caused by events or actions of the Armed Forces or Security Bodies or 
Forces in peacetime, the Insurance Compensation Consortium may seek information about 
the events from the competent judicial and administrative bodies.
2. RISK EXCLUDED
a)	Risks not eligible for compensation according to the Spanish Insurance Con­
tract Act.
b)	Risks caused to persons insured by an insurance contract differing from risks 
charged in favor of the Consorcio de Compensación de Seguros.
c)	Risks produced by armed conflict regardless of any official declaration of war.
d)	Risks derived from nuclear energy notwithstanding Law 12/2011 of May 27, on 
third-party liability for nuclear damages or those produced by radioactive ma­
terials.
e)	Risks caused by a phenomena of nature different from the ones contemplated 
in section 1.a) above and, specifically, risks caused by elevation of the phreatic 
level, mountainside movements, landslides, rock detachment and similar phe­
nomena, except when these are caused by rain that, in turn, has provoked ex­
traordinary flooding in the area and occurring simultaneously to said flooding.
f)	 Risks caused by riots during meetings and demonstrations taking place as con­
templated in Organic Law 9/1983 of July 15, regulating the right of assembly, and 
during legally organized strikes except when such actions may be classified as 
extraordinary events as specified in paragraph 1.b) above.
g)	Risks caused by bad faith on the part of the insured party.
h)	Risks caused by incidents arising before the first premium payment or when, as 
contemplated in the Insurance Contract Act, the coverage of the Insurance Com­
pensation Consortium is suspended or the insurance is terminated as a result of 
failure to pay the premiums.
